mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Strong payroll growth (PAYEMS) indicates robust corporate hiring and economic expansion, which drives equity market optimism and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns in high PAYEMS regimes fall below 0.5% in 40%+ of cases, or the series shows negative correlation with returns (Pearson < -0.1).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in residential construction permits (HOUST) signals weakening demand for housing, which correlates with negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns in declining HOUST regimes exceed 2% in 50%+ of cases, or the series shows positive correlation with returns (Pearson > 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: When the CPIAUCSL core inflation rate exceeds 2.5% year-over-year, central banks are forced to tighten policy, creating headwinds for equity markets and generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in high-inflation regimes exceed 1.5% in 40%+ of cases, or the series shows negative correlation with returns (Pearson < -0.1).

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous increase in industrial production (INDPRO) and decline in real sales (RSAFS) indicates overcapacity and weak demand, which drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in interaction regimes show positive correlation with the signal (Pearson > 0.1), or the combined effect fails to explain 15%+ of return variance (R² < 0.15).

mechanism_narrative: A 0.5% monthly decline in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) combined with rising payroll gains (PAYEMS) signals a tightening labor market, which drives positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 5-day returns in interaction regimes show negative correlation with the signal (Pearson < -0.1), or the combined effect fails to explain 10%+ of return variance (R² < 0.1).

mechanism_narrative: When GDP growth (GDPC1) exceeds 3% annualized and housing starts (HOUST) decline by more than 10% month-over-month, it signals a demand-driven market correction, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day returns in interaction regimes show positive correlation with the signal (Pearson > 0.1), or the combined effect fails to explain 20%+ of return variance (R² < 0.2).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in industrial production (INDPRO) above its 12-month moving average during a period of declining oil prices (not in dataset) would signal strong supply-side growth, but since oil prices aren't observable, this hypothesis is not actionable.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day returns in high INDPRO regimes fall below 0.8% in 50%+ of cases, or the series shows negative correlation with returns (Pearson < -0.1).
